nameMeanings.name: Pittman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Pittmann: from a compound personal name formed with Pitt, a short form of Peter + -mann ‘man’, or a variant of Bittmann .

variants.name: Pittmon
meanings: Altered form of   Pittman  , a surname of English and German origin.

variants.name: Pitman
meanings: English (mainly southwestern): nickname from Middle English pit, pit(t)e, put(te) ‘hollow, pit’ + man, signifying one who lived or worked at such a location. See Pitt .
Americanized form of German Pitmann: variant of Pittmann (see Pittman ).

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

en.lifeSummary: Anne Pittman was born in 1425, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with John Turman. She died in 1485, in her hometown, at the age of 60.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne Pittmanは1425年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアビショプ'ス ストルトフォードで生まれた。彼女はJohn Turmanとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1485年に故郷で60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Pittman(은)는 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Turman(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1485년 그녀의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
